mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of months where UNRATE > 8.5, or signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production contraction, supply-side bottlenecks and falling capacity utilization trigger short-term risk-off sentiment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months where INDPRO declines, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25%, extreme monetary accommodation creates asset price inflation dynamics that override fundamental valuation concerns, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in <50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.25, or the signal exhibits negative Rank IC in subsequent 12-month rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01, or 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates weak consumer-driven demand despite headline expansion, triggering short-term equity sell-offs over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5d returns in >60% of observed quarters, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise (p > 0.15) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, rising industrial production signals cost-push pressures rather than demand strength, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL > 220 and INDPRO rises, or the regime-specific signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in stratified backtests.
